messages: {host} promised one quick peek. Historically, that promise has the reliability of a group project.
Your work did not finish itself while this page loaded. Rude, but predictable.
One scroll becomes twenty, then suddenly you are watching a stranger organize their fridge.
This is probably not the boss fight you meant to start. The actual quest is still waiting.
Close this now and your to-do list might forgive you. It has been through a lot.
allowUsed: That pass is already spent today. The loophole department is closed.
Nope, that ticket was punched already. Back to the mission.
Allowance used. Your attention span filed a complaint.
kept: Good. The block stays. Proud of that tiny responsible click.
Excellent. The distraction trap remains locked.
unblocked: {host} is unblocked. Use this power like an adult.
The guardrail is gone for {host}. Do not make this weird.
{host} is free. Your tasks are still waiting.
confirmFirst: Unblock {host}? This is the part where the distraction pretends it is important.
Really unblock {host}? Be honest: is this useful, or just shiny?
Remove the block for {host}? Your focus was doing fine until this button appeared.
confirmSecond: Last chance. Are you choosing work, or choosing the dopamine vending machine?
Final check: will this help the task, or just help you avoid the task?
One more time: would future you approve, or sigh loudly?
